items.title: Komatsu expects profits to tumble 16% on Iran war, US tariffs
UCLA student government censured after condemning event with ex-hostage Omer Shem Tov
items.description: University, Jewish groups and a sitting regent denounce student leaders for claiming freed captive's talk 'amplified one-sided narrative that erases systems of oppression and occupation' The post UCLA student government censured after condemning event with ex-hostage Omer Shem Tov appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: Marlène Laruelle: 'Orban's defeat is a particularly strategic loss for Russia'
items.description: The setback for the former Hungarian prime minister, who fueled the Kremlin's narrative that Europe is reluctant to support Ukraine, weakens Vladimir Putin's plans, even though the battle against illiberalism is far from won, explains the historian and political scientist in an interview with Le Monde.

items.title: Continuing hostilities in Lebanon as 1.2 million displaced sink into despair
items.description: Despite a U.S.-brokered ceasefire, Israel has continued striking Lebanon and its troops are occupying a strip of the south, destroying homes they describe as Hezbollah infrastructure. Hezbollah, meanwhile, has kept up attacks against Israeli troops in Lebanon and on northern Israel. The continuing hostilities have caus…

items.title: Mosca, il 9 maggio si terrà una parata militare nella Piazza Rossa
items.description: Per commemorare l'81° anniversario della vittoria nella Seconda guerra mondiale

items.title: Northern Ireland makes arrest linked to suspected New IRA car bombing
items.description: The nationalist group has been escalating threats, nearly 30 years after a peace deal largely ended sectarian violence.

items.title: Their nation at war, Israelis still demand to know: How could Oct. 7 happen?
items.description: Amid wars with Iran and Hezbollah, Israelis still very much want an independent state commission of inquiry to learn the truth about the failure to prevent the Hamas attack that sparked the Gaza war. Israel’s top court has given the Netanyahu government more time.

items.title: Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns
items.description: Kremlin-controlled paramilitaries also alleged it inflicted ‘irreplaceable losses’ on insurgents avoiding civilian casualtiesRussia’s defence ministry has claimed its Africa Corps – the successor to the former Wagner mercenary group – prevented a coup in Mali over the weekend, avoiding mass civilian casualties and infl…

items.title: What next for Mali's junta after shock of rebel offensive?
items.description: The military came to power to bolster security but the weekend's attacks have brought that into question.

items.title: Kremlin Calls for Stability in Mali as Rebels Seize Kidal and Target Capital
items.description: Moscow said it did not know the current status or whereabouts of Assimi Goïta, the leader of Mali’s military junta.

items.title: North Korea Is Honoring Some Of Its Soldiers Killed In The Ukraine War. But What About The Rest?
items.description: Why a sprawling war memorial that was opened on April 26 commemorates only a fraction of the estimated thousands of troops Pyongyang lost in Russia.

items.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza ceasefire is ‘increasingly fragile’, UN warns
items.description: The Security Council turns again to the Middle East crisis in New York on Tuesday, with debate focusing on the fragile Gaza ceasefire, a worsening humanitarian crisis there and efforts to advance a US-backed peace plan.UN senior political affairs official Khaled Khiari warned of increasingly dire conditions in Gaza and…

items.title: Deadlock over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz cripples peace efforts
items.description: Two months after the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran started the war, peace talks are on hold, with control of the Strait of Hormuz and the future of Iran's nuclear program as the two main points of contention.

items.title: Mali junta destabilized by 'unprecedented' insurgency
items.description: After extremist attacks killed Mali's defense minister and hit several cities, the unrest now poses a serious challenge to the ruling junta, analysts say.

items.title: Two Dead in Kramatorsk as Russian Shelling Hits Residential Area
items.description: A woman and a man were killed while standing on the street during a morning strike on the Donetsk region city, which also damaged eight homes and wounded a 50-year-old civilian.
